title: Durable recovery for asynchronous export jobs

issues.title: Persist an idempotent export job state machine
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Retrying the same idempotency key returns the original job without duplicating work
AC-2: A process restart resumes queued and running jobs from the last durable transition
AC-3: Terminal failures retain a user-safe reason and the last verified checkpoint
issues.approach: Store each state transition with a monotonic revision and idempotency key. Workers claim a revision using compare-and-swap, write the export checkpoint before acknowledging work, and make terminal transitions immutable. This keeps retry behavior independent of worker lifetime and gives recovery code one authoritative state source.
test_hooks: Unit: duplicate idempotency key and illegal transition table
Integration: kill worker after checkpoint write and resume from durable revision
Regression: existing synchronous export path remains unchanged

issues.title: Expose bounded retry and recovery status
acceptance_criteria: AC-4: Status distinguishes retryable, terminal, and operator-blocked failures
AC-5: Retry requests require the current revision and reject stale clients
issues.approach: Return the durable state, revision, checkpoint summary, and a server-derived recovery action. Accept retries only for retryable states and require the caller's observed revision so an old page cannot revive superseded work.
test_hooks: Contract: every state maps to one explicit recovery action
Race: stale revision loses to a newer terminal transition

test_strategy.test_levels: Unit coverage for transition and idempotency rules; integration coverage for worker death, restart, and competing retries; contract coverage for status and recovery actions.
test_strategy.new_infrastructure: A deterministic fake clock and crashable in-memory worker harness; production persistence uses the existing job store.
test_strategy.regression_surface: Synchronous exports, job-store migrations, worker claim semantics, status polling, and error-message redaction.
test_strategy.open_questions: Confirm the retention period for terminal checkpoints before implementation; it changes storage sizing but not the state-machine contract.
